error.name: Error
error.message: semantic UX review failed with 2 blocker finding(s)

selected_findings.observed: Only age and gender are collected in this slice. No family mode, reminders, or item editing are included.
selected_findings.required: Replace visible internal language 'slice' with user-facing wording.

selected_findings.observed: Visible copy says: 'Only age and gender are collected in this slice. No family mode, reminders, or item editing are included.' This directly exposes internal slice/scope language and implementation boundaries instead of user-facing guidance.

Generating screen helper says: 'The plan is created locally before the dashboard appears.' This explains system mechanics rather than user value, which conflicts with the contract guidance for explanatory copy.

Welcome/onboarding lead copy includes phrases like 'self-only flow' and 'rule-based dashboard,' which sound product-internal and process-oriented rather than helping the user understand their benefit in plain language.

When a detail item cannot be loaded, the screen uses the generic heading 'Health item details' and does not show the item title fallback if available. This weakens recovery because users cannot easily tell which health item failed to open.

error.name: Error
error.message: semantic UX review failed with 2 blocker finding(s)

selected_findings.observed: The dashboard score section is labeled 'Read-only health score' and explained with 'This score comes only from the generated age and gender rules for this profile.' This tells system mechanics instead of what the score means for the user’s progress after completing an item.

Required detail content falls back to generic unfinished-product wording: 'Action details are not available yet.' and 'Timing is being prepared.' The contract requires safe, user-facing fallback copy; these do not help the user understand what to do or why.
The visible score label 'Read-only' is interaction-model language, not user-task language. It weakens the slice goal of showing immediate reflected progress in a clear, meaningful way.

error.message: Cannot run coder:repair-implementation-findings: docs/testing/SL-003-user-checklist.md result is unresolved; mark Status: Fail and document Manual QA Findings first

selected_findings.finding: After entering the user age and gender we land on the page that shows the button 'view full health plan'. when we click on any of the boxes in the 'All recommended preventive care steps' section, we see the details of that step, but no button to get back to anywhere. the only button is the 'Mark as Done' button. THe only way to get back is to use the browser back button.
selected_findings.expected: Button that allows us to go back from the step details to the full health plan view.
selected_findings.required_repair: Include a button taking us back to the health plan view.

selected_findings.finding: from the main page, when we click on an item and get its detailed view, we do get the 'Back to dashboard' button. when we click on it, it takes us back to the dashboard, but then the Health Plan section is missing entirely. If we do a refresh of the browser page, the Health plan section appears again.
selected_findings.expected: When we come back, the health plan section should be still available.

error.name: Error
error.message: semantic UX review failed with 2 blocker finding(s)

selected_findings.observed: The visible heading stack ('Full health plan' / 'Your complete preventive care plan') never makes clear that this is the plan for the active profile. For a per-profile plan view in a product that supports multiple profiles, the screen reads as a generic plan screen rather than a clearly profile-scoped one.

The totals helper says 'Open dashboard items: {count}. Completed items: {count}.' This introduces an extra 'Open dashboard items' concept tied to another surface instead of using the slice’s unified user-facing status model of Due, Planned, or Done. The wording is unclear to users and drifts into system/surface mechanics rather than meaningful plan status context.

selected_findings.observed: Past-date validation throws the visible message "Choose today or a future date," with a trailing comma, which is malformed user-facing copy and fails the requirement for valid human-readable states.

error.name: Error
error.message: semantic UX review failed with 1 blocker finding(s)

selected_findings.observed: The visible timing label returned for the custom reminder option is "Choose a date" via getReminderTimingLabel(), but the slice requires the user-facing option set to include "Custom date" alongside "In 1 month" and "In 3 months". This means one required reminder choice is not presented with the contracted wording/meaning.
selected_findings.required: Visible option label must be 'Custom date'.

error.name: Error
error.message: semantic UX review failed with 3 blocker finding(s)

selected_findings.observed: Unknown or missing status values resolve to the visible label "Due," and unknown priorities resolve to "Later." That is not a safe fallback; it can misstate the user's actual care state instead of showing neutral unknown-state copy.

Custom reminder date recovery is not handled with clear field-level guidance. The model can throw "A custom reminder date is required.", but the route only converts one specific error into adjacent validation and sends other custom-date failures to a generic save error, so a blank date does not get the plain-language inline recovery copy the slice requires.

When the user selects "Custom date," the primary action remains available unless saving is already in progress. This violates the slice UX contract that save should be blocked until a valid date is entered, so the action semantics are misleading at the moment of use.
